text.title: Subscription Models Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Subscription Models. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one model: SaaS | membership | replenishment | curation. Mixed models cause customer confusion + complex churn signals.
rule.rationale: Hidden mixed models cause complex unwinds + churn spikes.

rule.statement: Each tier gates feature(s) tied to a buyer outcome — not feature count. Random gating drives downgrades.
rule.rationale: Random feature gating produces non-strategic downgrades.

rule.statement: Track MRR (monthly), gross churn % (monthly), LTV (rolling 12). Without instrumentation, growth is blind.
rule.rationale: Un-instrumented growth hides churn until terminal.

rule.statement: Automatic dunning emails fire on card decline; manual recovery only after auto-3 cycles fail.
rule.rationale: Without dunning automation, recoverable failed-charge revenue is lost.

rule.statement: Lifecycle policy covers: trial-end, downgrade, cancel, win-back. Missing stages = customers fall through cracks.
rule.rationale: Missing lifecycle stages cause customers to fall through cracks.

rule.statement: Grandfather existing subscribers ≥6 months on tier or pricing changes. Forced migration causes churn spike.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ops-subscription-models.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
